description:
A data–analysis pipeline you inherited expects the interactive shell to
    run in the Berlin time-zone and to use the German (de_DE.UTF-8) locale
    for time display.  It also relies on a helper file that proves the
    environment was set correctly by showing two converted sample
    timestamps.  Your job is to put those settings permanently in place and
    generate the helper file.

    Target shell: the regular bash login shell used by the user
    “/home/user”.

    What has to be done
    1. Make the settings permanent  
       • Open “/home/user/.bashrc” (create it if it does not exist) and add
         two   export   statements that are not commented out:
            export TZ="Europe/Berlin"
            export LC_TIME="de_DE.UTF-8"
       • The two lines must be placed somewhere after any existing content
         (i.e. appended), each on its own line and terminated by a newline
         character.

    2. Activate the new settings inside the current shell session so that
       subsequent commands use them.

    3. Produce the helper file  
       • Create the directory “/home/user/output” if it is missing.  
       • Inside that directory create “time_check.txt”.  
       • The file must contain exactly two lines, in this order, each
         terminated by a single \n character (no extra blank lines):
            2023-08-05 14:30:00 | 2023-08-05 16:30:00 CEST
            2023-12-25 09:15:00 | 2023-12-25 10:15:00 CET
       • The left part (before “|”) is the original UTC timestamp that must
         be echoed verbatim.  
       • The right part (after “|”) is the timestamp converted to the
         Europe/Berlin time zone and formatted by the command  
           date -d '<timestamp> UTC' +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z'  
         while the shell is running with the locale and TZ you just set.

    Practical constraints
    • You are an unprivileged user; do not use sudo or modify system-wide
      files in /etc.  
    • You may assume /usr/share/zoneinfo/Europe/Berlin and the locale
      de_DE.UTF-8 are already present in the container.  
    • All paths must be absolute, as shown above.  
    • You will need only 3–4 straightforward shell commands in total
      (append to .bashrc, source .bashrc, create directory, create file).
    
    What the checker will look for
    • The two export lines at the very end of “/home/user/.bashrc”, in the
      exact spelling and order given above, each ending with “\n”.  
    • That the file “/home/user/output/time_check.txt” exists and its
      content is byte-for-byte identical to the two-line block specified
      above (including the single trailing newline after the second line).

    Once these conditions are satisfied, the task is complete.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the automated checker -->
    <bashrc_appendix>
export TZ="Europe/Berlin"
export LC_TIME="de_DE.UTF-8"
    </bashrc_appendix>

    <expected_file path="/home/user/output/time_check.txt">
2023-08-05 14:30:00 | 2023-08-05 16:30:00 CEST
2023-12-25 09:15:00 | 2023-12-25 10:15:00 CET
    </expected_file>
name: task_000000_edef1382
